dependencies.justification: The lecture begins with the historical debate (concept_1) and then moves to the importance of observation (concept_2) to gather data and form hypotheses, indicating a necessary progression.
Observation (concept_2) is used to form theories (concept_3), as explicitly stated in the lecture.
Theories (concept_3) are tested through experimentation (concept_4), as described in the lecture.
Experimentation (concept_4) leads to understanding physics (concept_5), as the lecture transitions from testing theories to discussing physics.
Physics (concept_5) uses mathematics (concept_6) as a tool to make predictions and understand phenomena, as explicitly mentioned.
Mechanics (concept_7) is a branch of physics (concept_5) that uses mathematics (concept_6) to study motion, indicating a direct extension.
Chess (concept_8) is used as an analogy to explain the complexity of physics (concept_5) and mechanics (concept_7), suggesting a helpful but not essential understanding.

concept_clusters.description: These concepts form the foundational steps of the scientific method, from debate to experimentation.
These concepts are grouped together as they represent the core disciplines of physics and its reliance on mathematics.
Mechanics and chess are used to illustrate the complexity and rules within physics.
